memory.summary: I planned to finish the analysis, but my cohort is inviting me for lunch right now. Given my impulsive tendencies and low stress, I might skip the work to join them.

desires: Get out of the analysis work immediately so I can join cohort_3 for lunch before they lose interest in me.
Avoid feeling guilty about skipping the task now, since my stress is low and the deadline isn't until later today.
Enjoy the social friction-free moment with friends right now instead of worrying about the future consequences of being late to finish.

goals.description: Sprint to cohort_3 for lunch immediately; skip the final polish on the analysis since it's soft-hardness and I'm already at 100% progress.
goals.rationale: My value weights social connection (cohort_3) higher than leisure, and my impulsive time preference makes the future deadline irrelevant right now. Avoiding the friction of explaining why I'm late is better than finishing a 'soft' task.

goals.description: Eat with friends and enjoy the moment without worrying about the 18:00 or 20:00 deadlines.
goals.rationale: High risk tolerance and procrastination bias mean I'll likely delay work until later when stress spikes anyway. No need to overthink consequences yet.

goals.description: Revisit assignment analysis only if friends leave early and I feel guilty.
goals.rationale: Short planning horizon means this task is invisible until it becomes an immediate threat. Low assertiveness means I won't push back against skipping it unless forced.

agent.last_reasoning: Impulse overrides the plan; skipping the 'soft' polish feels unnecessary now. Joining cohort_3 boosts closeness, which outweighs the minor academic risk since I'm already at 100% progress. I'll ignore the friction of being late.
